title: Solo Washing in Travel: A Framework for Evaluating Solo-Friendly Claims
description: This report introduces the concept of solo washing and proposes a framework for evaluating solo-friendly claims in travel products and services. It defines solo washing as a mismatch between solo-friendly marketing and actual product support, then develops a four-dimension evaluation model covering pricing, accommodation and design, transparency and communication, and user experience. The paper includes illustrative public examples and a comparative scoring table.
